Summarise physician age as median (IQR) text

Source: R/physician_age.R
mysterycall_physician_age.Rd

Computes the median, Q1, and Q3 of a numeric age column (ignoring NA) and returns a ready-to-paste sentence for manuscript methods or results sections.

Usage

mysterycall_physician_age(data, age_column)

Arguments

data

A data frame containing the age data.

age_column

Character scalar naming the numeric column in data that holds physician ages.

Value

A single character string of the form "The median age was XX.XX years (IQR: Q1.X--Q3.X years)." where the median is rounded to 2 decimal places and IQR bounds to 1.

Details

NA values in age_column are removed before computing quantiles (i.e., na.rm = TRUE). All quantiles use the default R type = 7 interpolation.

See also

mysterycall_impute_age() to derive age from graduation year; mysterycall_age_category() to bin ages into publication-ready groups.

Other provider characteristics: mysterycall_academic_patterns(), mysterycall_age_category(), mysterycall_assign_region(), mysterycall_check_academic_name_patterns(), mysterycall_classify_medical_school(), mysterycall_classify_practice_setting(), mysterycall_classify_ruca(), mysterycall_collapse_rare(), mysterycall_extract_physician_name(), mysterycall_genderize(), mysterycall_get_academic_indicators_summary(), mysterycall_government_patterns(), mysterycall_impute_age(), mysterycall_most_common_gender(), mysterycall_parse_certification_subspecialty(), mysterycall_recode_credentials(), mysterycall_reconcile_specialty(), mysterycall_reorder_by_freq()

Examples

# Example 1: Basic usage with a small dataset
data <- data.frame(age = c(30, 40, 50, 60, 35, 45, 55, 65))
summary_sentence <- mysterycall_physician_age(data, "age")
print(summary_sentence)
#> [1] "The median age of the dataset was 47.5 (IQR 25th percentile 38.8 to 75th percentile 56.2)."

# Example 2: Handling missing data
df_with_na <- data.frame(age = c(30, 40, NA, 60, 35, NA, 55, 65))
summary_sentence <- mysterycall_physician_age(df_with_na, "age")
print(summary_sentence)
#> [1] "The median age of the dataset was 47.5 (IQR 25th percentile 36.2 to 75th percentile 58.8)."

# Example 3: Different age distribution
df_large <- data.frame(age = c(rep(30, 70), rep(40, 30), rep(50, 20), rep(60, 10)))
summary_sentence <- mysterycall_physician_age(df_large, "age")
print(summary_sentence)
#> [1] "The median age of the dataset was 30 (IQR 25th percentile 30 to 75th percentile 40)."
